message:
fix(cli): use ResolveSessionWithOptions for JSON-aware session resolution and add input validation tests

The copy, save, and session-persist commands were calling
ResolveSession() which does not propagate the --json output mode flag,
causing interactive TUI prompts to fire even when JSON output was
requested (e.g. from scripted/piped invocations).

- Switch copy.go, save.go, and session_persist.go from ResolveSession()
  to ResolveSessionWithOptions() with TreatAsJSON: IsJSONOutput(), so
  that session resolution skips interactive prompts when the caller
  requested structured JSON output
- Add TestResolveEnsembleSessionRejectsInvalidSessionName in cli_test.go
  to verify that path-traversal session names like "../escape" are
  rejected at the ensemble resolution layer
- Add TestResolveSessionWithOptionsRejectsInvalidExplicitSessionName in
  util_test.go to verify the same validation at the
  ResolveSessionWithOptions entry point

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -162,7 +162,7 @@ func runCopy(w io.Writer, session string, filter AgentFilter, opts CopyOptions)
 
 	t := theme.Current()
 
-	res, err := ResolveSession(session, w)
+	res, err := ResolveSessionWithOptions(session, w, SessionResolveOptions{TreatAsJSON: IsJSONOutput()})
 	if err != nil {
 		return err
 	}

@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@ func runSave(w io.Writer, session, outputDir string, lines int, filter AgentFilt
 
 	t := theme.Current()
 
-	res, err := ResolveSession(session, w)
+	res, err := ResolveSessionWithOptions(session, w, SessionResolveOptions{TreatAsJSON: IsJSONOutput()})
 	if err != nil {
 		return err
 	}
